name: Local 802 - American Federation of Musicians
description_short: Local 802 AFM is a professional musicians' union offering member benefits including health care, pension, and a credit union for musicians in the entertainment industry.
description_long: Local 802 of the American Federation of Musicians (AFM) is the world's largest local union of professional musicians, based in New York City. Founded in 1951 (at least for their health fund), the organization represents recording, freelance orchestral, and performing musicians across the entertainment community. The union operates as a membership-based organization providing advocacy, collective bargaining, and member services. Local 802 offers a comprehensive suite of member benefits centered on financial security and professional support. These include a Musicians Health Fund providing health care and insurance, pension programs, a credit union for members, grants and emergency assistance, legal advice, scholarships, access to rehearsal space, and unclaimed check recovery services. Members also receive access to the Music Performance Trust Fund and gain collective bargaining power for improved pay, job security, and working conditions through union contracts. What distinguishes Local 802 is its hyper-specialization in serving professional musicians specifically, rather than functioning as a general-purpose financial institution. The organization combines traditional union representation with member financial services, making it uniquely positioned to understand the income volatility and professional challenges facing recording and performing artists. Their credit union operates exclusively for members, and they maintain active campaigns on contemporary issues like AI protection for musicians. However, Local 802 is fundamentally a union first and financial services provider second. Access to their banking products (credit union) and financial assistance is restricted to union members who meet eligibility requirements. The website provides limited detail about credit union rates, terms, or product specifics. For non-members seeking general banking, credit, or personal finance solutions, Local 802 is not accessible. Even for members, the union's primary function is advocacy and contract negotiation rather than comprehensive retail financial services.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Operates a dedicated credit union exclusively for member musicians with union-backed financial products
Established Musicians Health Fund since 1951 providing health insurance and healthcare access to members and families
Offers emergency relief funding and assistance programs specifically designed for musicians experiencing financial hardship
Provides legal advice services to members at no additional cost beyond union membership
Maintains unclaimed checks database and recovery services for members owed money from prior performances
All-in-one member support including pension, grants, scholarships, and rehearsal space access
Active advocacy on contemporary issues affecting musicians' financial interests, including AI copyright protection
cons: Credit union and financial products available only to union members—not accessible to general public
Website provides minimal specific details about credit union product terms, interest rates, or lending requirements
No information on membership eligibility criteria, initiation fees, or annual dues costs
Limited scope as a financial institution compared to full-service banks or credit unions serving broader populations
Primary organizational focus is union representation and advocacy, not consumer financial services
best_for: Professional recording musicians seeking specialized financial services and health benefits through union membership
Freelance orchestral and performing artists needing emergency financial assistance and income support
Musicians looking for collective bargaining power to improve pay and working conditions in contracts
Entertainment industry professionals seeking legal advice and representation on music-related matters
diagnosis: Local 802 is best for professional union musicians seeking specialized financial products, health insurance, and collective advocacy—not for general consumers seeking retail banking or credit products. Access is strictly limited to union members, making it unavailable for the broader public, and detailed financial product specifications are largely absent from their public website.
services: Credit union membership and banking services for union members
Musicians Health Fund health insurance and healthcare coverage
Pension programs and retirement planning for professional musicians
Emergency relief and financial assistance grants
Scholarship programs for musicians and music students
Unclaimed checks recovery and payment services
Collective bargaining contract negotiation and administration
Monthly Allegro newsletter with industry updates and contract information
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Local 802 AFM is a musicians' union offering health care, pension, and credit union benefits for entertainment industry members.
